title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: Welcome looplook, versatile R/Bioconductor toolkit developed integrate three-dimensional (3D) chromatin architecture data (e.g., HiChIP, ChIA-PET, Hi-C) tabular omics datasets, including transcriptomics, chromatin accessibility, protein-DNA interactions (derived ChIP-seq, CUT&Tag, CUT&RUN), genetic variants annotated genome-wide association studies (GWAS). Numerous studies demonstrated distal regulatory elements physically interact target gene promoters via 3D chromatin looping, thereby regulating expression genes located tens kilobases megabases away along linear genome. However, conventional annotation strategies typically assign putative regulatory elements (e.g., informed ChIP-seq peaks) nearest neighboring genes cis, often deviates actual biological mechanisms. Consequently, accurate assignment non-coding genetic variants orphan regulatory peaks cognate target genes represents major bottleneck functional annotation genomic regulatory elements. overcome challenge, looplook systematically prioritizes experimentally validated spatial chromatin contacts batch-annotate thousands regulatory elements genome-wide, high-throughput scale, thereby identifying candidate target genes high confidence unprecedented efficiency. Beyond utility integrative tool target gene annotation, looplook serves standalone platform dedicated chromatin loop analysis. Even absence auxiliary omics data, tool enables systematic annotation 3D chromatin interactome , classification complex spatial interaction topologies (e.g., Enhancer-Promoter, Promoter-Promoter interactions), quantification node connectivity, facilitates identification dense regulatory hubs enhancer cliques (e.g., super-enhancers) drive cell-type-specific transcriptional programs.

title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: “3D Spatial Bridge” Multi-Omics: Integrates auxiliary genomic features (e.g., GWAS risk SNPs, ChIP-seq peaks) 3D chromatin loops. implements rigorous “Smart Fallback” logic prioritizes distal loop-mediated target genes reverting nearest neighboring genes spatial interactions detected, ensuring comprehensive gapless target annotation. Comprehensive Loop Annotation & Topological Hub Detection: addition loop mapping, looplook enables biologically informed annotation 3D chromatin interactome. helps categorize spatial interaction types computes spatial node degrees identify candidate 3D regulatory hubs. Expression-Aware Refinement: Incorporates quantitative expressional data filter spurious structural contacts. chromatin regions may exhibit enhancer promoter activities, looplook provides function reclassify loop anchors associate transcriptionally silent reference promoters enhancer-like elements (eP), yielding refined network regulatory interactions. Reproducible Consolidation & Multi-source Consensus: Utilizes graph-theoretic clustering effectively harmonize biological replicates mitigate potential technical noise. framework can used identify multi-source consensus integrating datasets various experimental designs, HiChIP assays targeting different factors (e.g., H3K27ac Pol II). enables orthogonal validation facilitate construction high-confidence, unified 3D chromatin interactome based consistent spatial features. Automated Multi-Omics Functional Interpretations: Provides end--end analytical engine generating publication-grade visualizations functional interpretations. includes assessments network connectivity expression dynamics, transcription factor motif enrichment (SeqLogos), pathway association networks (Cnetplots).

code: if (!requireNamespace("devtools", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("devtools") if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("BiocManager")  # Install annotation databases required for human (hg38) analysis BiocManager::install(c("TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene", "org.Hs.eg.db"))  # Install looplook from GitHub devtools::install_github("zying106/looplook") library(looplook) # Create a temporary directory to store all output plots and Excel files out_dir <- tempdir()

title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: 3D genomics analyses, individual replicates typically exhibit certain degrees inconsistency noise. consolidate_chromatin_loops function serves foundational data-cleaning module, merging multiple replicates standardized, unified 3D chromatin interaction coordinate framework. module identifies multi-source consensus integrating datasets varied experimental designs, HiChIP assays targeting H3K27ac Pol II. capturing shared spatial interactions across inputs, framework enables orthogonal validation mitigate potential technical artifacts assay-specific biases. approach leverages consistent spatial features provide robust foundation downstream analyses supported multiple lines experimental evidence.

title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: "consensus" (Recommended): Implements graph-based connected component analysis cluster spatially proximal anchors across samples. retains clusters detected ≥ min_consensus biological replicates. "intersect": Enforces strict reference-based filtering, retaining loops show full genomic overlap reference file (File 1). "union": Retains chromatin interactions across entire cohort, ideal exploratory pan-tissue analyses. min_consensus: using "consensus" mode, parameter defines minimum number biological replicates loop cluster must detected retained final dataset. set NULL, algorithm dynamically computes strict majority threshold (e.g., ≥ 75% replicates). gap: Defines maximum allowable spatial distance (base pairs) loop anchors considered part physical cluster (default: 1000). min_raw_score acts pre-filter applied individual BEDPE files clustering (e.g., removing singleton noise PET count < 2) substantially reduce computational memory overhead. min_score serves post-filter applied final merged chromatin interactome ensure high-confidence interactions. blacklist_species: Automatically excludes chromatin loops overlapping high-variance, artifact-prone genomic regions (e.g., centromeres, telomeres) integrating official ENCODE blacklist specified species (e.g., "hg38", "mm10"). region_of_interest: Accepts auxiliary BED file (e.g., specific disease-associated locus ChIP-seq peak set) exclude global background interactions, outputting loops physical connectivity target genomic region.

what: Example 1: Building a Global Consensus Interactome
title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: represents standard workflow generating high-confidence, whole-genome 3D chromatin interaction dataset via integration biological replicates removal blacklist-associated artifacts.
code: # Locate example BEDPE replicates f1 <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_1.bedpe", package = "looplook") f2 <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_2.bedpe", package = "looplook") global_out <- file.path(out_dir, "consensus_loops_global.bedpe")  # Execute consensus merging with strict Quality Control consensus_global <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(   files = c(f1, f2),   mode = "consensus",   gap = 1000,   min_raw_score = 2, # Pre-filter sequencing noise   blacklist_species = "hg38", # Apply artifact blacklist   out_file = global_out )

title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: scenarios, researchers interested 3D chromatin interactions associated specific biological features, active enhancers (marked H3K27ac) particular transcription factors. providing BED file region_of_interest, tool enables efficient “--fly” capture, retaining loops whose anchors overlap specified functional regions.
code: # Locate a BED file containing H3K27ac ChIP-seq peaks h3k27ac_peaks <- system.file("extdata", "example_k27ac_peaks.bed", package = "looplook") targeted_out <- file.path(out_dir, "H3K27ac_anchored_loops.bedpe")  # Execute consensus merging, strictly capturing H3K27ac-associated loops consensus_targeted <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(   files = c(f1, f2),   mode = "consensus",   gap = 1000,   min_raw_score = 2,   region_of_interest = h3k27ac_peaks, # Surgical capture via 1D features   out_file = targeted_out )

what: Example 3: Multi-source Consensus for Orthogonal Validation
title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: integrative multi-omics analyses, orthogonal validation across independent functional assays critical identifying robust 3D regulatory interactions. instance, enhancer-promoter (E-P) loops typically supported spatial co-localization H3K27ac (marking active chromatin) RNA polymerase II (indicative transcriptional machinery engagement). utilizing "consensus" mode intersect independent loop datasets, looplook facilitates identification chromatin interactions supported multi-source evidence. rigorous intersection strategy effectively mitigates assay-specific technical biases artifacts, yielding refined set topological hubs couple physical chromatin architecture active transcriptional regulation.
code: loop_k27ac <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_H3K27ac.bedpe", package = "looplook") loop_pol2 <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_pol2.bedpe", package = "looplook") dual_functional_out <- file.path(out_dir, "Dual_Functional_Consensus.bedpe")  consensus_dual <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(   files = c(loop_k27ac, loop_pol2),   mode = "consensus",   gap = 1000,   min_raw_score = 2,   out_file = dual_functional_out )

title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: module constitutes core mapping engine looplook. Using annotate_peaks_and_loops, users can classify topological architecture interactome. Optionally, users may supply target_bed (e.g., GWAS loci, eQTLs, ChIP-seq peaks) trace non-coding regulatory signals putative functional target genes. resolve mapping ambiguities within densely populated gene loci, single loop anchor overlaps multiple candidate genes, engine implements rigorous three-step hierarchical annotation pipeline: Expression Pre-filter: Eliminates transcriptionally silent genes based user-provided expression matrix. Functional Biotype Prioritization: Prioritizes remaining candidates functional class following order: Protein Coding > lncRNA > Pseudogene. Dominant Expression Tiebreaker: Designates gene highest transcriptional abundance target gene resolve remaining mapping ambiguities.

previous_headings: Module 1: Data Consolidation & Preprocessing > Example 3: Multi-source Consensus for Orthogonal Validation
title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: multi-omic integration relies several key parameters: bedpe_file: 3D structural framework (e.g., high-confidence consensus interactome generated Module 1). target_bed: auxiliary genomic features interest (e.g., BED file GWAS SNPs, ATAC-seq peaks, ChIP-seq peaks) awaiting spatial target assignment. expr_matrix_file & sample_columns: Optional strongly recommended. expression matrix (e.g., RNA-seq) enables Expression Pre-filter Tiebreaker logic, substantially reducing false-positive gene assignments. species: Specifies genome assembly (e.g., "hg38", "mm10"). engine automatically loads corresponding Bioconductor TxDb org.db annotations. 0 (Default): Restricts annotation direct physical contacts (Anchor ↔︎ Anchor B). 1 (Hub Mode): Evaluates secondary network effects within enhancer cliques (Anchor ↔︎ Anchor B ↔︎ Anchor C, function enables topological linkage C). tss_region: Defines genomic window surrounding transcription start site (TSS) used define promoter regions (default: c(-3000, 3000) bp).

what: Example A: Integrative Analysis (Loops + Genomic Features + RNA-seq)
title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: represents comprehensive “3D Spatial Bridge” scenario. links external genomic feature (peaks) 3D‑inferred target genes, simultaneously resolving ambiguous assignments dense genomic regions weighing gene expression.
code: # Locate auxiliary genomic features and RNA-seq expression matrix expr_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_tpm.txt", package = "looplook") atac_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_peaks.bed", package = "looplook") if (requireNamespace("TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene", quietly = TRUE) &&   requireNamespace("org.Hs.eg.db", quietly = TRUE)) {   res_integrated <- annotate_peaks_and_loops(     bedpe_file = global_out, # Uses the global consensus network from Module 1     target_bed = atac_path, # External genomic features     expr_matrix_file = expr_path, # Activates the rigorous 3-step tiebreaker     sample_columns = c("con1", "con2"),     species = "hg38",     neighbor_hop = 0, # Focus on direct physical contacts     hub_percentile = 0.95, # Top 5% nodes defined as hubs     out_dir = out_dir,     project_name = "Example_HiChIP_Integrative"   ) }

what: Example B: Intrinsic Loop Profiling (Loops ONLY)
title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: users focused exclusively 3D interactome architecture without auxiliary data related genomic features, looplook functions standalone platform loop profiling structural hub identification.
code: if (requireNamespace("TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene", quietly = TRUE) &&   requireNamespace("org.Hs.eg.db", quietly = TRUE)) {   res_loops_only <- annotate_peaks_and_loops(     bedpe_file = global_out,     target_bed = NULL, # Omit genomic features entirely     expr_matrix_file = expr_path, # Resolves multi-gene conflicts at loop anchors     sample_columns = c("con1", "con2"),     species = "hg38",     neighbor_hop = 0,     out_dir = out_dir,     project_name = "Example_Loops_Only"   ) }

Gene-Centric (promoter_centric_stats) Total_Loops / n_Linked_Promoters / n_Linked_Distal: Deconstructs gene’s 3D connectome. High n_Linked_Distal suggests potential regulation enhancer clusters, whereas high n_Linked_Promoters may indicate possible participation multi-gene transcription. Dominant_Interaction: predominant topological class governing gene (e.g., E-P vs. P-P). Is_High_Connectivity_Gene (Distal variant): Binary flags defined hub_percentile prioritize putative 3D regulatory hubs. B. Enhancer-Centric (distal_element_stats) Target_Genes: Promoters physically contacted distal anchor. n_Linked_Promoters: Facilitates identification candidate master enhancers contact multiple distinct genes. Is_High_Connectivity_Distal_Element: Flags highly connected non-coding elements may act 3D hubs.

title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: refine_loop_anchors_by_expression function implements functional filtration via following key parameters: annotation_res: Foundational 3D annotation output generated Module 2. expr_matrix_file & sample_columns: Quantitative expression matrix (e.g., TPM, FPKM) specific replicates average, establishing robust baseline expression profile. threshold & unit_type: Defines quantitative expression cutoff (e.g., threshold = 1, unit_type = "TPM") required consider gene biologically active. parameter enables downward compatibility various normalization methods. reclassify_by_expression: transformative logical parameter (TRUE/FALSE). enabled, transcriptionally silent Promoters (P) Gene Bodies (G) simply discarded; instead, reclassified enhancer-like regulatory elements (eP, eG). correction refines regulatory topology (e.g., reclassifying functionally silent P-P loop curated eP-P loop).

title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: section contains critical routing parameters define biological scope stringency downstream analyses: "targets" (Variant/Peak-Centric): Focuses exclusively putative genes regulated user-defined genomic features (e.g., GWAS SNPs, ATAC-seq peaks). "loops" (Global Network-Centric): Analyzes full 3D interactome, including genes associated spatial loops, without considering user-defined genomic features. c("loops", "targets"): Analyzes scopes simultaneously, strongly recommended comparative functional profiling. "" (Default): Retains broad 3D regulatory targets, including distal enhancers looping gene bodies. "promoter": Applies stringent filtering, requiring loops anchor explicitly canonical promoter regions. TRUE (Hybrid Mode): Utilizes comprehensively merged annotation (_Filled columns Module 2). prioritizes 3D loop-derived targets rescuing unlooped genomic features (peaks) assigning nearest genes, ensuring data loss complete functional overview. FALSE (Pure Spatial Mode): Highly stringent. keeps 3D loop-derived targets clear annotation. use_nearest_gene: Logical parameter. TRUE, pipeline bypasses 3D loop-based assignment uses nearest neighboring gene, serving conventional baseline reference Control comparisons.

what: Example B: Peak-Driven Strict Promoter Profiling (High Stringency)
title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: mode applies rigorous dual-filtering strategy yield high-confidence target gene set. First, target_source = "targets" restricts profiling genes regulated specifically user-defined genomic features (e.g., GWAS SNPs TF binding sites), excluding background spatial loops. Second, target_mapping_mode = "promoter" enforces topological stringency: loops linking peaks target genes must directly contact canonical reference promoters (.e., strict E-P P-P loops), excluding permissive connections enhancer-gene body (E-G) interactions.
code: if (exists("refined_res")) {   res_B <- profile_target_genes(     annotation_res = res_integrated,     diff_file = diff_path,     lfc_col = "log2FoldChange",     expr_matrix_file = expr_path,     metadata_file = meta_path,     target_source = "targets", # 1. Focus exclusively on inputted genomic features     target_mapping_mode = "promoter", # 2. Require strict loop-to-promoter collision     include_Filled = TRUE,     use_nearest_gene = FALSE,     project_name = "Scenario_B_StrictPromoter",     out_dir = out_dir,     run_go = FALSE   ) }

title: Looplook: An integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: visualize local spatial interactome high resolution, module generates IGV-style multi-tiered genomic browser view via plot_peaks_interactions function. visualization integrates multi-omics datasets three distinct, non-overlapping genomic tracks, enabling intuitive assessment 3D structural context: Loop Track (Top): Depicts 3D chromatin interactions Bezier arcs connecting paired spatial anchors. score_to_alpha = TRUE enabled, opacity (alpha channel) arc scaled proportionally quantitative interaction score. Target Track (Middle): Renders user-provided genomic features (e.g., ChIP-seq peaks GWAS SNPs) input BED file, facilitating direct visualization spatial overlap loop anchors. Gene Track (Bottom): Retrieves longest canonical transcript gene using standard Bioconductor annotation databases, exons introns mapped according strand directionality. Overlapping gene models vertically separated avoid label collisions improve readability.

title: Integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: Welcome looplook, versatile R/Bioconductor toolkit developed integrate 3D chromatin architecture data (e.g., HiChIP, ChIA-PET, Hi-C) tabular omics datasets, including transcriptomics, chromatin accessibility, protein-DNA interactions (derived ChIP-seq, CUT&Tag, CUT&RUN), genetic variants annotated genome-wide association studies. Numerous studies demonstrated many distal regulatory elements physically interact target gene promoters via 3D chromatin loopings, thereby regulating expression genes located tens kilobases megabases away linear genome. However, conventional annotations tend assign putative elements (peaks) nearest genes cis, often fails reflect biological reality. Hence, accurate assignment non-coding genetic variants orphan peaks cognate target genes remains major bottleneck target annotation functional elements. address , looplook systematically prioritizes physical spatial chromatin contacts batch-annotate thousands regulatory elements genome-wide, high-throughput scale, thereby identifying candidate target genes high confidence unprecedented efficiency. Beyond utility tool integrative target annotation, looplook can used standalone utility loop analysis per se. Even absence auxiliary omics data, systematically annotates 3D chromatin interactome , classifying complex spatial topologies (e.g., Enhancer-Promoter, Promoter-Promoter interactions) quantifying node connectivity uncover dense regulatory hubs enhancer cliques (e.g., super-enhancers) drive cell-type-specific transcriptional programs.

title: Integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: 3D genomics analyses, individual replicates typically exhibit certain degrees inconsistency noise. consolidate_chromatin_loops function serves foundational data-cleaning module, merging multiple replicates standardized, unified 3D chromatin interaction coordinate framework. Key Parameters: mode: Defines overarching merging algorithm. "consensus" mode (recommended) employs graph-based connected component analysis cluster nearby chromatin loop anchors across biological/technical samples. "intersect" mode applies strict reference-based filtering retain overlapping interactions, "union" mode retains detected interactions exploratory pan-tissue analyses. min_raw_score & min_score (Dual-Filter): min_raw_score acts pre-filter applied individual BEDPE files clustering (e.g., removing singleton noise substantially reduce computational memory overhead). min_score serves post-filter applied final merged chromatin interactome ensure high-confidence interactions. gap: Defines maximum spatial distance (base pairs) allowed loop anchors consideration part physical cluster. blacklist_species: Automatically excludes chromatin loops overlapping high-variance, artifact-prone genomic regions (e.g., centromeres, telomeres) integrating official ENCODE blacklist specified species (e.g., "hg38", "mm10"). region_of_interest: Accepts auxiliary BED file (e.g., specific disease-associated locus ChIP-seq peak set) exclude global background interactions, outputting loops physical connectivity target genomic region.
code: library(looplook) out_dir <- tempdir()  # Execute consensus merging with strict Quality Control consensus_global <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(   files = c("rep1.bedpe", "rep2.bedpe"),   mode = "consensus",   gap = 1000,   min_raw_score = 2,           # Pre-filter sequencing noise   blacklist_species = "hg38",  # Apply ENCODE artifact blacklist   out_file = file.path(out_dir, "consensus_loops.bedpe") )

title: Integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: module serves core target mapping engine. seamlessly executes rigorous hierarchical pipeline resolve locus assignment conflicts: Expression Pre-filter → Functional Biotype Prioritization → Dominant Expression Tiebreaker. Key Parameters: target_bed: pecifies auxiliary genomic features interest (e.g., GWAS SNPs, ATAC-seq peaks, transcription factor binding sites) require spatial target gene assignment. expr_matrix_file & sample_columns: Providing RNA-seq matrix allows engine activate Expression Pre-filter Tiebreaker logic, drastically reducing false-positive gene assignments genomic regions harboring multiple genes. neighbor_hop: advanced topological parameter 3D chromatin interactome network traversal. value 0 restricts annotation direct physical chromatin interactions ; value 1 (Hub Mode) extends analysis secondary network effects within enhancer cliques. tss_region: Defines spatial boundary gene promoters relative Transcription Start Site (TSS). Output Data Dictionary: Comprehensive 3D Spatial Catalog module systematically exports multi-layered tabular catalog (e.g., *_Basic_Results.xlsx) detailing spatial interactome: Integrative Target Mapping (target_annotation) Delineates spatial coverage genomic variants/features inputted user. strictly differentiates Assigned_Target_Genes (target genes identified exclusively via 3D physical chromatin loops) *_Filled columns, implement “Smart Fallback” algorithm assign nearest linear genes unlooped loci, ensuring comprehensive, gap-free annotation coverage. 3D Network Architecture (loop_annotation) Resolves biological syntax structural interactome. classifies topological interactions (loop_type, e.g., E-P, P-P) distinguishes biologically relevant Putative_Target_Genes broader, raw physical interaction footprints (All_Anchor_Genes). Topological Hub Detection Quantifies structural node degrees (e.g., n_Linked_Promoters, n_Linked_Distal) rigorously deconstruct 3D interactome two complementary perspectives: promoter_centric_stats: Identifies core target genes regulated complex regulatory architectures (e.g., enhancer arrays transcription factories)，distal_element_stats highlights high-connectivity non-coding regions facilitate discovery putative enhancer cliques. Figure 1: Representative outputs 3D-Guided Annotation. composite plot displays curated subset automated profiling suite, featuring macro-scale chromosomal ideograms topological overlap analysis annotated 3D interactome.
code: # Map auxiliary genomic features to 3D loops and resolve multi-gene conflicts res_integrated <- annotate_peaks_and_loops(   bedpe_file = file.path(out_dir, "consensus_loops.bedpe"),   target_bed = "atac_peaks.bed",   expr_matrix_file = "rna_tpm.txt",   sample_columns = c("con1", "con2"),   species = "hg38",   neighbor_hop = 0,   hub_percentile = 0.95,   out_dir = out_dir,   project_name = "Integrative_Annotation" )

title: Integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: Physical proximity structural prerequisite, direct proxy active transcriptional regulation. module integrates quantitative transcriptome data systematically filter transcriptionally silent physical chromatin contacts, ensuring functionally relevant 3D interactions retained. Key Parameters: threshold & unit_type: Defines quantitative cutoff (e.g., threshold = 1.0, unit_type = "TPM") required consider gene biologically active. parameter enables downward compatibility various normalization methods. reclassify_by_expression: enabled (TRUE), transcriptionally silent promoters simply discarded; instead, biologically reclassified enhancer-like regulatory elements. correction refines regulatory topology (e.g., reclassifying functionally silent P-P loop curated eP-P loop). Output Data Dictionary: Functionally Active Regulome Following transcriptome integration, refined tabular outputs represent high-confidence, functionally active subset initial 3D chromatin interactome. key features output follows: Expression-Aware Filtration: resulting catalog explicitly excludes pure structural chromatin loops lacking functional transcriptional output, yielding rigorously curated, functionally active regulatory network. Dynamic Topological Reclassification: topological annotations within loop_type biologically recalibrated. dynamically reclassifying transcriptionally silent promoters (P) enhancer-like elements (eP), catalog fundamentally corrects spatial regulatory syntax (e.g., seamlessly transforming transcriptionally silent P-P loop curated eP-P interaction axis). Figure 2: Representative outputs Expression-Aware Refinement. shown Multi-Omics Sankey Tracking, curated visualization dynamically traces fate peak 3D chromatin topological interactions corresponding transcriptionally active target genes.
code: # Expression-Aware Reclassification (Recommended) refined_res <- refine_loop_anchors_by_expression(   annotation_res = res_integrated,   expr_matrix_file = "rna_tpm.txt",   sample_columns = c("con1", "con2"),   threshold = 1,   unit_type = "TPM",   reclassify_by_expression = TRUE,    out_dir = out_dir,   project_name = "Refined_Network" )

title: Integrative suite for target assignment and functional annotation of chromatin interactions
text: module provides fully automated, end--end multi-omics analysis pipeline integrates 3D genomic interactions transcriptomic data unveil regulatory mechanisms targets interest. Key Parameters: "targets": Focuses exclusively putative target genes regulated inputted genomic features (Peak-Centric mode). "loops": Evaluates entire 3D interactome independently inputted genomic features (Global Network-Centric mode). target_mapping_mode Controls stringency 3D target assignment. "" accepts broad 3D target regulation, "promoter" highly stringent, requiring 3D loops explicitly anchor canonical promoter regions, excluding distal E-G connections. TRUE (Hybrid Mode): Utilizes comprehensively merged annotation, prioritizing 3D loop-derived target genes rescuing unlooped genomic elements assigning nearest linear genes. FALSE (Pure Spatial Mode): Strictly isolates analyzes 3D interactome. use_nearest_gene (Control) Serves classical baseline reference. set TRUE, engine bypasses 3D spatial topology strictly assigns genomic features nearest linear genes, facilitating direct comparison demonstrate novel functional insights gained 3D-guided mapping. Figure 3: Representative outputs Functional Profiling. curated composite highlights Divergent Concept Networks Asymmetric Motif Signatures, offering partial glimpse downstream visualizations decode trans-regulatory logic spatial hubs.
code: # Comprehensive Integrative Profiling (RNA-seq + Spatial Hubs + JASPAR + GO/PPI) res_profile <- profile_target_genes(   annotation_res = refined_res,   diff_file = "deseq2_res.txt",   lfc_col = "log2FoldChange",   expr_matrix_file = "rna_tpm.txt",   metadata_file = "sample_metadata.txt",   target_source = c("loops", "targets"),   target_mapping_mode = "all",   include_Filled = TRUE,   use_nearest_gene = FALSE,   project_name = "Functional_Profiling",   out_dir = out_dir,   run_motif = TRUE,    run_go = TRUE,   run_ppi = TRUE )

what: A core analytical engine enabling high-resolution spatial mapping of genomic features/peaks to 3D chromatin interaction targets — annotate_peaks_and_loops
title: A core analytical engine enabling high-resolution spatial mapping of genomic features/peaks to 3D chromatin interaction targets — annotate_peaks_and_loops
text: highly sophisticated, dual-purpose 3D genomic engine designed : Annotate Chromatin Loops: Accurately classify 3D spatial interactions (e.g., Enhancer-Promoter, Promoter-Promoter) using strict structural hierarchy. Map External Features (genomic features 3D): Act "3D Spatial Bridge" link auxiliary 1D genomic features (e.g., GWAS risk SNPs, ATAC-seq peaks, ChIP-seq binding sites) genuine regulated target genes, fundamentally outperforming simple "nearest gene" heuristics. Core Philosophy: Bridging genomic features-3D Gap Traditional annotations typically assign non-coding variants nearest linear gene. engine prioritizes physical 3D chromatin contacts. GWAS SNP enhancer lands anchor looping distal gene, distal gene accurately assigned. spatial loop exists, engine intelligently falls back nearest active local gene (Smart Fallback), ensuring comprehensive gapless annotation coverage. Key Algorithmic Innovations: Biotype & Expression-Aware Conflict Resolution: anchor overlaps multiple promoters (e.g., dense gene loci bidirectional promoters), executes rigorous 3-step resolution: Expression Pre-filter: Eliminates transcriptionally silent genes based user-provided expression matrix. Functional Biotype Prioritization:: Prioritizes remaining candidates functional class following order: Protein Coding > Antisense > lncRNA > Pseudogene. Dominant Expression Tiebreaker: Designates gene highest transcriptional abundance target gene resolve remaining mapping ambiguities. Dynamic Topology Control (neighbor_hop): Controls network diffusion depth. Allows signal propagation SNP -> Enhancer -> Hub -> Target Gene, ideal uncovering multi-way super-enhancer cliques. Hub Detection: Calculates precise node degrees identify high-connectivity Promoter Distal hubs based hub_percentile.

title: A core analytical engine enabling high-resolution spatial mapping of genomic features/peaks to 3D chromatin interaction targets — annotate_peaks_and_loops
text: bedpe_file Character. Path BEDPE file containing 3D loop coordinates ("3D Bridge"). target_bed Optional character. Path auxiliary BED file (e.g., GWAS summary stats, eQTLs, ChIP-seq/ATAC-seq peaks). provided, maps 1D regulatory regions 3D target genes. species Character. Reference genome build. Supported: "hg38", "hg19", "mm10", "mm9". tss_region Numeric vector. Defines promoter window around TSS. Default: c(-2000, 2000). out_dir Character. Directory saving generated PDF plots Excel results. expr_matrix_file Optional character. Path normalized RNA-seq matrix (e.g., TPM/FPKM). Highly recommended fuels intelligent conflict resolution filters silent elements. sample_columns Character vector Integer indices. Specifies columns expr_matrix_file average baseline expression reference. project_name Character. Prefix output files plot titles. color_palette Character. Color brewer palette name visualizations (e.g., "Set2"). karyo_bin_size Numeric. Bin size Karyotype genomic density heatmaps. Default: 1e5 (100kb). neighbor_hop Integer. Network Diffusion Depth. 0 (Default) captures direct physical connections ; 1 captures indirect 1-hop hub-mediated connections. hub_percentile Numeric (0-1). Top percentile threshold defining high-connectivity "Regulatory Hubs" (default: 0.95).

title: A core analytical engine enabling high-resolution spatial mapping of genomic features/peaks to 3D chromatin interaction targets — annotate_peaks_and_loops
text: invisible list comprehensive data frames (also auto-saved multi-sheet .xlsx): target_annotation: Detailed annotation target_bed. Features important column: Assigned_Target_Genes_Filled (Loop-prioritized target, gracefully falling back local nearest gene unlooped). loop_annotation: fully annotated 3D network. Features Putative_Target_Genes capturing precisely resolved regulatory targets. promoter_centric_stats: Gene-level topological statistics. Contains structural node degrees identifies Is_High_Connectivity_Gene. distal_element_stats: Enhancer-level topological statistics, highlighting critical regulatory anchors orchestrating multiple promoters. anchor_annotation: Locus-level summarization 1D anchor footprints.

what: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops
title: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops
text: function consolidates chromatin loops multiple BEDPE files. designed two main purposes: Replicate Consolidation: Merging biological technical replicates identify high-confidence, reproducible loops (e.g., 3 replicates H3K27ac HiChIP). Multi-Omics Integration: framework can used identify multi-source consensus integrating datasets various experimental designs, HiChIP assays targeting different factors (e.g., integrating H3K27ac H3K4me3, overlapping Hi-C ChIA-PET). function supports three modes: "consensus": Implements graph-based connected component analysis cluster spatially proximal anchors across samples. retains clusters detected ≥ min_consensus biological replicates. "intersect": Enforces strict reference-based filtering, retaining loops show full genomic overlap reference file (File 1). "union": Retains chromatin interactions across entire cohort, ideal exploratory pan-tissue analyses. also supports two-stage filtering strategy maximize signal--noise ratio: Pre-filtering (min_raw_score): Removes low-confidence noise (e.g., singleton reads) raw files merging. Post-filtering (min_score): Filters final consensus loops based aggregated score (e.g., average intensity).

title: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops
code: consolidate_chromatin_loops(   files = NULL,   gap = 1000,   mode = c("consensus", "intersect", "union"),   min_consensus = NULL,   min_raw_score = NULL,   min_score = NULL,   blacklist_species = NULL,   region_of_interest = NULL,   out_file = NULL )

title: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops
text: files Character vector. Paths BEDPE files (least two). gap Numeric. Distance (bp) consider loops overlapping. Default 1000. mode Character. Choose one following: "consensus", "intersect", "union". Merge strategy: "intersect": Strict reference-based filtering (keeps loops File 1 supported files). "union": Merges detected loops comprehensive map. "consensus": Graph-based clustering find consensus set supported majority samples. (Formerly "reproducible"). min_consensus Integer. Minimum number replicates loop must appear (effective mode = "consensus"). NULL (default), threshold automatically calculated: 2 replicates: Requires (2/2). >2 replicates: Requires strict majority (>75\ (e.g., 3 N=3, 4 N=4, 4 N=5). min_raw_score Numeric. Pre-filtering threshold. Loops raw score (e.g., read count) value individual files discarded merging intersection. Recommended value: 2 (remove singleton noise loops count=1). Default: NULL (pre-filtering). min_score Numeric. Post-filtering threshold. Minimum score keep consolidated loop merging. "consensus" mode, filters consensus loops based representative score. Default: NULL (post-filtering). blacklist_species Character. Species/build built-blacklist (e.g., "hg38", "hg19", "mm10", "mm9"), path custom BED file. region_of_interest Character. Path BED file. loops overlapping regions kept. out_file Character. file name (including file path) saving results extended BEDPE format.

title: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops

title: Consolidate and Integrate Chromatin Loops from Multiple Sources — consolidate_chromatin_loops
code: # 1. Get paths to example BEDPE files included in the package f1 <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_1.bedpe", package = "looplook") f2 <- system.file("extdata", "example_loops_2.bedpe", package = "looplook")  # 2. Run consolidation (ensure files exist) if (f1 != "" && f2 != "") {   # Example A: Intersect Mode   # Only keeps loops present in f1 that are also supported by f2   res_intersect <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(     files = c(f1, f2),     mode = "intersect",     gap = 1000,     out_file = tempfile(fileext = ".bedpe")   )    # Example B: Consensus Mode (formerly Reproducible)   # Finds consensus loops supported by both replicates (default for N=2)   res_consensus <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(     files = c(f1, f2),     mode = "consensus",     gap = 1000,     out_file = tempfile(fileext = ".bedpe")   )    # Example C: Union Mode   # Merges all loops into a single map   res_union <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(     files = c(f1, f2),     mode = "union",     gap = 1000,     out_file = tempfile(fileext = ".bedpe")   )    # Example D: Dual Filtering Strategy (Recommended for HiChIP)   # 1. Pre-filter: Discard singletons (score < 2) to remove noise.   # 2. Merge: Find loops present in both replicates.   # 3. Post-filter: Keep only strong consensus loops (score > 5).   res_clean <- consolidate_chromatin_loops(     files = c(f1, f2),     mode = "consensus",     min_raw_score = 2, # Pre-filter (remove noise)     min_score = 5, # Post-filter (keep strong loops)     gap = 1000,     out_file = tempfile(fileext = ".bedpe")   )    # Inspect results   length(res_intersect)   length(res_clean) } #> >>> Reading BEDPE files #>     File 1: 1000 loops #>     File 2: 1000 loops #> >>> Intersect mode: Reference-based filtering (No Coordinate Merging) #>     Base: File 1. what: End-to-end functional annotation pipeline integrating JASPAR transcription factor motif analysis, gene ontology enrichment, and protein-protein interaction network analysis — profile_target_genes
title: End-to-end functional annotation pipeline integrating JASPAR transcription factor motif analysis, gene ontology enrichment, and protein-protein interaction network analysis — profile_target_genes
text: function serves fully automated, end--end multi-omics analysis pipeline. elegantly bridges 3D genomic interactions (e.g., Hi-C, HiChIP) transcriptomic data (RNA-seq) systematically characterize functional landscape regulatory mechanisms underlying identified target genes. suite integrates multiple analytical modules: Differential Expression (LFC) Profiling: Compares Log2 Fold Change target genes genomic background using tailored violin/boxplots. GSEA Analysis: Evaluates rank-distribution custom target gene sets within global differential expression landscape. Expression & 3D Connectivity Dynamics: Generates Z-score expression heatmaps correlates 3D structural connectivity (e.g., Hub degree) gene expression/LFC using scatter sophisticated Raincloud plots. TF Motif Analysis: Scans JASPAR core motifs across proximal distal loop anchors, generating motif enrichment rank scatter plots sequence logos (SeqLogos). Functional Enrichment (GO): Performs Gene Ontology (BP) enrichment, visualizing results divergent concept networks (cnetplot) aggregated faceted lollipop charts. Interaction Networks (PPI): Constructs visualizes high-confidence Protein-Protein Interaction networks via STRING database.

Path normalized expression matrix (Genes x Samples, e.g., TPM FPKM). metadata_file Character. Path sample metadata file. Must contain "SampleID" "Group" columns. target_source Character vector. Source target genes analyze. Options include "loops" (genes connected specific loops) "targets" (genes overlapping external target BED). target_mapping_mode Character. Specifies mapping strategy target genes. Must one : "" (Default): Accepts physically connected 3D targets (including Gene Bodies). "promoter": Strictly enforces 3D loop must explicitly anchor Promoter region. loop_types Character vector. specific loop types analyze (e.g., c("E-P", "P-P")). Active "loops" included target_source. include_Filled Logical. TRUE, utilizes comprehensively merged gene assignment (retaining loop-assigned targets local host genes). use_nearest_gene Logical. TRUE, bypasses 3D loop-based gene assignment strictly uses nearest 1D gene (SYMBOL), serving spatial baseline reference. group_order Character vector. Optional factor levels sort sample groups visualizations. project_name Character. Prefix output files plot titles. out_dir Character. Directory path saving output PDF plots CSV tables. org_db Character. Organism annotation database (e.g., "org.Hs.eg.db" human, "org.Mm.eg.db" mouse). run_motif Logical. Whether perform Transcription Factor Binding Site (TFBS) motif analysis proximal distal anchors. genome_id Character. Reference genome assembly motif sequence extraction (e.g., "hg38", "hg19", "mm10"). motif_p_thresh Numeric. P-value threshold motifmatchr scanning. motif_ntop Numeric. Number top enriched motifs output SeqLogos. run_go Logical. Whether perform Gene Ontology (GO) ORA enrichment. run_ppi Logical. Whether construct Protein-Protein Interaction networks via STRING database (requires internet access). ppi_score Numeric. Minimum combined confidence score STRING interaction edges (0-1000). ppi_nSample Numeric. Maximum number top variable/LFC genes include PPI network prevent visual overcrowding. heatmap_nSample Numeric. Maximum number highly variable genes plot expression heatmap. gsea_nSample Numeric. Maximum number target genes sample GSEA maintain computational efficiency. cnet_nSample Numeric. Number top GO terms display divergent concept network plot. stat_test Character. Statistical test LFC comparisons ("wilcox.test" "t.test"). cor_method Character. Method sample correlation matrices ("pearson" "spearman").

title: End-to-end functional annotation pipeline integrating JASPAR transcription factor motif analysis, gene ontology enrichment, and protein-protein interaction network analysis — profile_target_genes
code: # ========================================================================= # 1. Get paths to real example files included in the package # ========================================================================= rdata_path <- system.file("extdata", "analysis_results.RData", package = "looplook") expr_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_tpm.txt", package = "looplook") diff_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_deg.txt", package = "looplook") meta_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_coldata.txt", package = "looplook")  # Check if all necessary files exist before running if (rdata_path != "" && expr_path != "" && diff_path != "" && meta_path != "") {   # Safely load the pre-computed annotation result from RData   temp_env <- new.env()   load(rdata_path, envir = temp_env)   raw_annotation <- temp_env[[ls(temp_env)[1]]]    out_base <- tempdir()    # =========================================================================   # Scenario A: Comprehensive Integrative Profiling (Loops + Targets)   #   # Application:   # Best for complete HiChIP + ATAC-seq (or ChIP-seq) joint analysis.   # It maps genes regulated by BOTH 3D distal loops and 1D direct target overlaps.   # =========================================================================   res_A <- profile_target_genes(     annotation_res = raw_annotation,     diff_file = diff_path,     lfc_col = "log2FoldChange",     expr_matrix_file = expr_path,     metadata_file = meta_path,     target_source = c("loops", "targets"),     target_mapping_mode = "all",     include_Filled = TRUE,     use_nearest_gene = FALSE,     project_name = "Scenario_A_Integrative",     out_dir = out_base,     run_go = FALSE, # Set to TRUE in real analysis     run_ppi = FALSE   )    # =========================================================================   # Scenario B: Peak-Centric & Promoter-Strict Profiling   #   # Application:   # Focuses ONLY on 1D peaks ("targets") and STRICTLY restricts the mapping   # to genes where peaks fall directly on their "promoter".   # Ideal for baseline epigenetic profiling, ignoring distal 3D regulation.   # =========================================================================   res_B <- profile_target_genes(     annotation_res = raw_annotation,     diff_file = diff_path,     lfc_col = "log2FoldChange",     expr_matrix_file = expr_path,     metadata_file = meta_path,     target_source = "targets", # Only 1D targets     target_mapping_mode = "promoter", # Strict promoter mapping     include_Filled = TRUE,     use_nearest_gene = FALSE,     project_name = "Scenario_B_PromoterOnly",     out_dir = out_base,     run_go = FALSE,     run_ppi = FALSE   )    # =========================================================================   # Scenario C: Highly Conservative Distal Enhancer-Targeting   #   # Application:   # Focuses on 3D loops. By setting `use_nearest_gene = TRUE` and   # `include_Filled = FALSE`, it forces the selection of strictly the nearest   # genes to the anchors, preventing the expansion of gene lists from broader   # annotated regions. Ensures a highly conservative gene target list.   # =========================================================================   res_C <- profile_target_genes(     annotation_res = raw_annotation,     diff_file = diff_path,     lfc_col = "log2FoldChange",     expr_matrix_file = expr_path,     metadata_file = meta_path,     target_source = "loops", # Only 3D loops     target_mapping_mode = "all",     include_Filled = FALSE,     use_nearest_gene = TRUE, # Strictly nearest gene     project_name = "Scenario_C_StrictLoops",     out_dir = out_base,     run_go = FALSE,     run_ppi = FALSE   ) } #> >>> Analysis Init | Root Project: Scenario_A_Integrative #> --- Reading files... #> Loaded Promoter Centric Stats. #>  #> ================================================================ #> >>> Processing Source: [loops] #>      Loops: Successfully located 'Putative_Target_Genes' #>  #> --- Task: EP_Genes (Valid Genes: 36) --- #>     Saved LFC Violin Plot: /tmp/RtmpLFv4cM/Scenario_A_Integrative_loops_EP_Genes_LFC_Violin.pdf #>     Saved GSEA Plot (Custom): /tmp/RtmpLFv4cM/Scenario_A_Integrative_loops_EP_Genes_GSEA.pdf #>     [Extra Analysis] Found 'n_Linked_Distal'. Running distal connectivity analysis... #>  #> --- Task: PP_Genes (Valid Genes: 124) --- #>     Saved LFC Violin Plot: /tmp/RtmpLFv4cM/Scenario_A_Integrative_loops_PP_Genes_LFC_Violin.pdf #>     Saved GSEA Plot (Custom): /tmp/RtmpLFv4cM/Scenario_A_Integrative_loops_PP_Genes_GSEA.pdf #>     [Extra Analysis] Found 'n_Linked_Distal'. title: A transcriptome-informed filtering framework to refine the regulatory element (anchor) classification and the loop-target linkage using gene expression profiles — refine_loop_anchors_by_expression
text: Core Algorithmic Innovations: Multi-Gene Parsing & Precision Filtration: Flawlessly handles merged gene assignments (e.g., "GeneA;GeneB") generated upstream biotype-aware annotations. splits, individually evaluates expression threshold, securely recombines surviving active targets. Biologically-Driven Downgrading (eP / eG): purely physical Promoter (P) loop anchor exhibits active transcription biologically reclassified enhancer-like element (eP). Gene bodies without transcription become eG. corrects regulatory syntax (e.g., turning silent P-P loop functionally accurate eP-P enhancer-promoter loop). Topological Hub Inheritance (Crucial): Unlike naive filters recalculate node degrees scratch, engine inherits foundational 3D Hub classifications (e.g., Is_High_Connectivity_Gene) raw physical data. structural hub remains structurally important; function simply annotates functional activation state, preventing network collapsing due tissue-specific silencing. Automated Target Purification: Intelligently detects external mapping columns (e.g., Assigned_Target_Genes_Filled) filters based expression, ensuring auxiliary BED targets strictly linked functionally verified active genes.

code: # 1. Get paths to the required example files in the package rdata_path <- system.file("extdata", "analysis_results.RData", package = "looplook") expr_path <- system.file("extdata", "example_tpm.txt", package = "looplook")  # 2.
